questions.question: Explain the main difference between a Domain Name System (DNS) and a Name Service (NS).
questions.hint: DNS is a protocol for resolving domain names, while NS is a service that delegates domain name resolution.

questions.question: Describe the purpose and functioning of a Recursive Name Server (RNS) in the DNS hierarchy.
questions.hint: RNS is used to resolve hostnames to IP addresses by making recursive queries to authoritative Name Servers.

questions.question: What is the role of a Top-Level Domain (TLD) in the DNS system, and how does it affect domain name resolution?
questions.hint: TLD is the highest level of the DNS hierarchy, and it affects domain name resolution by providing additional context for the domain name.
